steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content:
The underlined portion reads: "the broad chronological scope of Shurin's volume may make it seem more comprehensive than Mirrorshades."

The word may signals this is a perception that can be challenged. The sentence immediately pivots with but to argue that Mirrorshades is actually the more authoritative anthology. The underlined portion's job is to set up that reversal.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It supports the assertion that Leiber and Satifka should be considered essential figures in the cyberpunk movement.': The underlined portion says nothing about the importance of individual authors — it concerns the anthology's scope. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It indicates a sensible perception of a cyberpunk anthology that can nonetheless be disputed for a good reason.': The scope may make the anthology seem more comprehensive (sensible perception), but the "but" clause disputes this in favor of Mirrorshades (good reason). Exact match. ✓
  • Choice C — 'It suggests that, because they are relatively up to date, more recently published collections of cyberpunk stories are likely to be more inclusive than earlier anthologies.': The underlined portion makes no general claim about recency across multiple anthologies — it describes one specific volume's chronological breadth. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It helps explain why two compilers of anthologies have different ideas regarding which short stories should be labeled as "cyberpunk."': The text is about comprehensiveness vs. authority, not about labeling disagreements between compilers. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
